name: an E vitamin
annotation: The term Vitamin-E (tocochromenols) is a collective term for the eight forms of Tocotrienols tocotrienols and Tocopherols tocopherols, each existing as α, β, γ, and δ species. Structurally tocotrienols and tocopherols differ only in the degree of unsaturation of their side-chain. The tocotrienol chain comprises three trans-double bonds, whereas the tocopherol chain is fully saturated. In addition, the α, β, γ, and δ species of both compound groups vary in the methylation pattern of the chromanol ring . Vitamin E compounds are synthesized only by photosynthetic organisms, although they are essential dietary components for animals (including humans), where they act primarily as antioxidants. Their presumed role in plants involve protection of chloroplast lipids and chlorophylls against oxidative damages. The tocotrienols are mostly found in monocots and a few dicots , whereas tocopherols are wider spread in the plant kingdom and dominate in the dicots .

name: a [chondroitin-sulfate] containing 6-O-sulfo-N-acetylgalactosamine
annotation: Chondroitin-sulfates "Chondroitin sulfate" is a glycosaminoglycan found mostly attached to proteins as part of a proteoglycan. Chondroitin sulfate is composed of repeating disaccharide units composed of a hexuronic acid and a hexose sugar, specifically CPD-12521 and CPD-12557 linked by β(1 -> 3) linkages. Both units are often sulfated. This compound stand for CPD-12557 sulfated at the 6' position within the context of Chondroitin-sulfates.

name: a galactomannan
annotation: Mannans are polymers composed of β(1→4)-linked MANNOSE residues. Pure mannans are rarely found, and most contain α(1,6)-linked galactopyranosyl side chains. The extent of galactosylation varies greatly, and the mannose : galactose ratio is one of the main characteristics of the polymer as it determines their physicochemical properties, such as solubility in water, density, and the viscosity of the solution . On one hand, slightly galactosylated linear mannans (about 4% galactose), have been isolated from the seed endosperm of vegetable ivory nut (TAX-115507) and date (TAX-42345). They are water-insoluble, resemble cellulose in conformation of the individual molecular chains, and occur in cellulose microfibrils of the plant tissues . On the other hand, heavily galactosylated polymers (up to 96%) are abundant in the cell walls of storage tissues (endosperm, cotyledons, perisperm) of many seeds. Those from the endosperm of some leguminous seeds, such as guar (TAX-3832), locust bean or carob (TAX-20340), and tara gum (TAX-191904), are widely used commercially . Another important feature is the distribution of the galactopyranose residues along the mannan backbone, which affects the molecular interactions of the chains among themselves (self-association) and with other polysaccharides (cooperative associations) in solution .

name: a type 5 histo-blood group antigen precursor disaccharide
annotation: The histo-blood group antigen precursor disaccharides are found at the terminal end of oligosaccharide chains attached to proteins or sphingolipids, and serve as the actual substrates for various glycosyltransferases that terminate the chain elongation process, forming the ABH or Lewis antigens. Five types of precursor disaccharides are known to carry ABH activity - type 1 through type 4 and type 6. Type 1 precursors are widely expressed on endodermally-derived tissues such as lining epithelia and glandular epithelia. Type 2 precursors are found mainly on ecto- or mesodermally-derived tissues, including erythrocytes . Type 3 and 4 precursors are found on ecto- or endodermally-derived tissues, including the salivary glands and kidneys . Since ABH antigens are present not only on erythrocytes, but also on various organs such as the salivary glands and kidneys , they are called histo-blood group ABH antigens. Six types of precursor disaccharides have been described: beta-Gal-13-beta-GlcNac-R: β-D-galactosyl-(1→3)-N-acetyl-β-D-glucosaminyl-R B-Gal-14-NacGlc-R: β-D-galactosyl-(1→4)-N-acetyl-β-D-glucosaminyl-R beta-Gal-13-alpha-GalNac-R: β-D-galactosyl-(1→3)-N-acetyl-α-D-galactosaminyl-R beta-Gal-13-beta-GalNac-R: β-D-galactosyl-(1→3)-N-acetyl-β-D-galactosaminyl-R beta-Gal-13-beta-Gal-R: β-D-galactosyl-(1,3)-β-D-galactosyl-R beta-Gal-14-beta-Glc-R: β-D-galactosyl-(1,4)-β-D-glucosyl-R

name: a phytoalexin
annotation: Phytoalexins are antimicrobial substances synthesized de novo by plants that accumulate rapidly at areas of incompatible pathogen infection. Phytoalexins do not form a coherent chemical class - they are chemically diverse, with different types characteristic of particular plant species.

Both units are often sulfated. This compound stand for CPD-12557 (non sulfated) within the context of Chondroitin-sulfates.

name: a ketose
annotation: A ketose is a monosaccharide containing one LONG-CHAIN-KETONE ketone group per molecule. Ketoses can isomerize into Aldoses (in which the carbonyl group is located at the end of the molecule). Such ketoses are known as reducing sugars.

name: a polymyxin
annotation: Polymyxins are antibiotics with a general structure consisting of a cyclic peptide with a long hydrophobic tail. They disrupt the structure of the bacterial cell membrane by interacting with its phospholipids. Polymyxins are produced by the Gram-positive bacterium TAX-1406 and are selectively toxic for Gram-negative bacteria due to their specificity for the lipopolysaccharide molecules that exist within many Gram-negative outer membranes.

name: a pyrrolidine
annotation: Pyrrolidines are heterocyclic amines that have a saturated five-membered ring.

name: a δ amino acid
annotation: A δ amino acid is a non-proteinogenic amino-acid in which the amino group is located on the carbon atom at the δ position from the carboxy group.

name: a red algal galactan
annotation: Red algal galactans are sulfated polysaccharides having usually a linear backbone built up of alternating 3-linked β-D-galactopyranose and 4-linked α-galactopyranose residues. The β-galactose residues always belong to D-series, whereas the α-galactose residues are D in carrageenans and L in agars. A substantial part of the α-galactose residues may exist in the form of a 3,6-anhydro derivative .

name: a guanidine
annotation: Guanidines are organonitrogen compounds containing a carbamimidamido (guanidino) group.

name: adenosylcob(III)alamin-[corrinoid adenosyltranferase]
annotation: Cobalamin is the term used for Cobamides where the cobalt ion is attached to a DIMETHYLBENZIMIDAZOLE lower axial ligand, without specifying the upper axial ligand. It includes several compounds often referred to as vitamin B12, that differ in the upper axial ligand. The functional forms of the B12 vitamin, CPD-9037 and ADENOSYLCOBALAMIN, include either a methyl group or an 5'-deoxyadenosyl group as their upper axial ligand, respectively. Other forms of cobalamin include CPD0-1256 (or its conjugated acid AQUACOBIIIALAMIN) and CPD-315. CPD0-1256 Hydroxocobalamin is a naturally produced form synthesized by many bacterial species, while CPD-315 is a common semi-synthetic form produced from bacterially-synthesized CPD0-1256. Vitamin B12 pills often contain CPD-315, which is converted to the active forms in the body. The cobalt ion embedded in cobalamin may exist in different charges (+1, +2 or +3), and the different forms of the compound are referred to as COB-I-ALAMIN, CPD-1829 and CPD-17105, respectively. Spectroscopic analyses of EC-2.5.1.17, have revealed that the enzyme binds its cofactor in the "base-off" conformation (in which the lower axial DMB base does notr form a bond with the cobalt atom) in both the Co2+ and Co3+ oxidation states .

name: a tetraterpenoid
annotation: Terpenoids Terpenoids make up a very large family of natural products containing more than 50,000 structurally diverse compounds. They are categorized by the number of C5 isoprene units as follows: Hemiterpenes Hemiterpenoids (one C5 unit), Monoterpenes monoterpenoids (C10; two C5 units), Sesquiterpenes sesquiterpenoids (C15; three C5 units), Diterpenes diterpenoids (C20; four C5 units), Sesterterpenes sesterterpenoids (C25; five C5 units), Triterpenes triterpenoids (C30; six C5 units), C35-Terpenes "sesquarterpenoids" (C35; seven C5 units), and Tetraterpenes tetraterpenoids (C40; eight C5 units). Most of the tetraprenoids known are the carotenoids, which are derived from the acyclic parent, CPD1F-114 (also named ψ,ψ-carotene). An exception is a family of compounds that are derived from CPD-20653.

name: [dermatan] containing N-acetyl-D-galactosamine
annotation: Dermatan-Sulfate "Dermatan sulfate" is a glycosaminoglycan found mostly in skin but also in blood vessels, the heart valves, tendons, and the lungs. Dermatan sulfate is composed of repeating disaccharide units composed of a hexose sugar and a hexuronic acid, many of which sulfated. The most common disaccharide unit in dermatan sulfate is composed of CPD-12515 and CPD-12557 (many are sulfated) linked by β(1 -> 3) linkages. This compound stand for CPD-12557 (non sulfated) within the context of Dermatan-Sulfate.

name: an alkaloid
annotation: Alkaloids are naturally occurring heterocyclic compounds that contain nitrogen and an alkaline pH (hence the name). Alkaloids are secondary metabolites, and have a marked physiological action on animal physiology. They are produced by a large variety of organisms, including bacteria, fungi, plants, and animals.

name: a type II plant arabinogalactan
annotation: Arabinogalactans (AGs) are widely spread throughout the plant kingdom. Many edible and inedible plants are rich sources of these polysaccharides. AGs are associated with the pectin cell-wall component by physical bonds, and some of them are covalently linked to the complex pectin molecule as neutral side chains . AGs have been classified into two general forms, named Plant-Arabinogalactans-I "type I" and Plant-Arabinogalactans-II "type II". AGs of type I have a linear β-(1→4)-D-Galp backbone, while those of type II have a β-(1→3)-D-Galp backbone. In type II AGs, known also as arabino-3,6-galactan, the (β-1→3)-D-Galp backbone is heavily substituted at position 6 by mono- and oligosaccharide side chains composed of arabinosyl and galactosyl units. It is more widespread than the AG type I and occurs in cell walls of dicots and cereals often linked to proteins (known as arabinogalactan proteins). AG type II, along with galactomannans and cellulose, comprises the predominating polysaccharides of green arabica coffee beans . In some case type II AG contains a small amount of β-glucuronate. There are many variations of AG type II produced by different plants. Two forms that have been studied thoroughly are Gum-Arabic "gum arabic AG", which is produced by acacia trees, and Larch-AG "larch AG, produced by larch trees (members of the genus TAX-3325). AG is very abundant in the wood of larch trees - certain parts of the western larch (TAX-3327) contain up to 35% AG . Larch AG type II is located in the lumen of the tracheids and ray cells, and is thus not a cell-wall component and by definition, not part of the Hemicelluloses of these trees. AG type II also occurs as minor, water-soluble components in softwoods. The glycan structure shown here is adapted from .

name: a farnesol
annotation: FARNESYL-PP (FPP) has been recognized as a key intermediate in the biosynthesis of more complex sesquiterpenoids, higher terpenoids, and steroids for many years, and more recently many other roles have been discovered for both the pyrophosphate form and the corresponding alcohol farnesol. For example, farnesol acts as a quorum-sensing molecule to suppress filamentation in the fungus TAX-5476 , and farnesyl derivatives are involved in the a-factor mating peptide of the dodecapeptide pheromone found in TAX-4932 . Terpene alcohols such as farnesol have antibacterial effects against TAX-1280, including promotion of potassium ion leakage . Furthermore, FPP plays a pivotal role in the posttranslational processing of Ras proteins. Farnesol has 4 isomer, the most abundant of which is 2-TRANS6-TRANS-FARNESOL .

name: an XXLG xylogulcan
aliases: a xyloglucan with galactose side chain at position 3
annotation: Xyloglucan Xyloglucan is the major hemicellulose in dicotyledon type I cell walls. The cross-linking between xyloglucan chains is a major factor in the stability of the three-dimensional matrix between adjacent cellulose microfibrils . Xyloglucan has a backbone of β-1,4-linked cellotetraose units, which are substituted in C-6 by α-D-xylosyl residues at two or three of the first three glucosyl residues, but not in the position closest to the reducing end . Some of the xylosyl residues are subsequently further substituted by (1,2)-β-D-galactosyl residues at the second and/or third xylose residue and by (1,2)-α-L-fucose at the galactosyl unit of the third xylose residue . A common terminology uses G for basic non-substituted glucosyl units within a xylosylglycan, X for glucose residues substituted by α-D-xylose, L for glucose residues substituted by α-D-xylose and β-D-galactose, and F for glucose residues substituted by α-D-xylose, β-D-galactose and α-L-fucose . Most xylosylglucans consist of repeating heptasaccharide Xyloglucans-Xylose XXXG units.

name: an aggrecan cleavage product
annotation: Aggrecan is a proteoglycan, a core protein modified with carbohydrates. The human core protein, encoded by the gene ACAN, is very large (over 250 kDa). Along with Type-II collagen, aggrecan forms a major structural component of cartilage, particularly articular cartilage. Aggrecan consists of two globular structural domains at the N-terminal end and one globular domain at the C-terminal end, separated by a large domain heavily modified with glycosaminoglycans. The two main modifier moieties are themselves arranged into distinct regions, a Chondroitin-sulfates region and a KERATAN-6-SULFATE region. Certain proteases such as EC 3.4.24.80 and EC 3.4.24.82 are able to cleave bonds in the interglobular domain of aggrecan.

name: a 3-O-acetylated rhamnogalacturonan I
annotation: Rhamnogalacturonans-I "Rhamnogalacturonan I " is a polysaccharide in the Pectin pectin family, consisting of repeating α-(1,2)-L-rhamnose-α-(1,4)-D-GALACTURONATE disaccharide units. About half of the Rha residues are substituted with side chains of various neutral oligosaccharides such as arabinans, galactans, and arabinogalactans. Most pectic substances are furthermore esterified with acetyl or methyl groups at some of the galacturonate residues in the backbone.

name: a macrolide antibiotic
annotation: Macrolide-Antibiotics "Macrolide antibiotics", such as Erythromycins erythromycin, CPD-13808, CPD-13809, and CPD-13810, constitute a well-established class of antimicrobial agents used for the treatment of respiratory tract, urinary tract, skin, and soft tissue infections. As the name implies, these drugs contain a macrolide or lactone ring, which can be 14-, 15-, or 16-membered. Attached to these rings are a number of unusual 2,6-dideoxy- or 2,3(4),6-trideoxysugars, such as CPD-13780. Macrolide antibiotics are effective inhibitors of bacterial protein biosynthesis. They bind to the large 50S ribosomal subunit at or near the peptidyl transferase center and block the elongation of the peptide chain.

name: a salmochelin
annotation: Salmochelins are C-glucosylated siderophores related to enterobactin that are produced by Salmonella species, uropathogenic and avian pathogenic Escherichia coli strains, and certain Klebsiella strains. Mono-, di-, and triglucosylated forms have been reported . The glucosylation has been interpreted as a bacterial evasion mechanism against the mammalian catecholate siderophore-binding protein siderocalin.

name: an oxidized factor 420
annotation: One of the distinguishing features of methanogenic bacteria is their blue-green fluorescence when irradiated with UV light. This fluorescence is due to F420, a flavin-related redox-active compound that is used as a cosubstrate by many enzymes in these organisms. While it is well-known for its role in methanogenesis, factor 420 has also been found in various eubacteria of the actinobacteria class, such as Streptomyces, Rhodococcus, Nocardioides, and Mycobacterium, where it assists in the synthesis of antibiotics like tetracycline and lincomycin. In addition, a modified form of the factor is also used by cyanobacteria in the repair of DNA damage. Factor 420 (F420), named for its intense absorbance at 420 nm, was originally isolated by Cheeseman et al , and later identified by Eirich et al as the N-(N-L-lactyl-γ-L-glutamyl)-L-glutamic acid phosphodiester of 7,8-didemethyl-8-hydroxy-5-deazariboflavin 5'-phosphate . The PWY-8112 "F420 biosynthetic pathway" produces the intermediate CPD-17242 "F420-0", which contains no glutamate side chains. Glutamates are then added by several enzymes. F420 molecules isolated from different organisms differ in the number of the glutamate molecules in the side chains, as well as the nature of the bond (which could be either α or γ) .

name: [keratan]-galactose
annotation: KERATAN-6-SULFATE "Keratan sulfate" is a glycosaminoglycan found mostly in the cornea, cartilage, and bone. It is a linear polymer that consists of a repeating disaccharide unit. The basic repeating disaccharide unit consists of GALACTOSE and N-ACETYL-BETA-D-GLUCOSAMINE, either of which can be sulfated at carbon position 6. This compound stand for CPD-3604 (non-sulfated) within the context of KERATAN-6-SULFATE.

name: a [SoxY protein]-L-cysteine
annotation: SOXYDENIT-MONOMER is a sulfur covalently-binding protein, which binds sulfur anions, including sulfur, sulfite, thiosulfate and sulfate, to form a SoxY-thiocysteine-S-sulfur compound adducts . Unlike many other sulfur-carrier proteins, these sulfur anions are not simply bound to a cysteine residue, but to an S-sulfane adduct . SOXYDENIT-MONOMER combines with SOXZDENIT-MONOMER, a sulfur compound chelating protein, to form the CPLX-881 complex. A thiosulfate ion can be transferred the free thiol group of a conserved cysteine residue (Cys138) of SOXYDENIT-MONOMER to form SoxY-Thiocysteine-Sulfate, the first covalently bound sulfur adduct in the sulfur oxidation pathway of TAX-82367. The reaction is catalyzed by the CPLX-7149 complex . The attachment of sulfite to the same cysteine residue, which produces SoxY-Systeine-Sulfinate, is not dependent on CPLX-7149 . The residues of SOXYDENIT-MONOMER, SOXZDENIT-MONOMER, and SOXBDENIT-MONOMER involved in the interactions among them have been identified . In native gradient polyacrylamide gels CPLX-881 sometimes appears as a 50-kDa α2β2 heterotetramer . Neither SoxY nor SoxZ contains a cofactor or metal.

name: a 2'-deoxyribonucleoside
annotation: Nucleosides are glycosylamines consisting of Bases bound to a CPD0-1110 ribose or 2-DEOXYRIBOSE sugar via a β-glycosidic linkage. The nucleosides are called Ribonucleosides ribonucleosides when the sugar is CPD0-1110 ribose, and Deoxy-Ribonucleosides deoxyribonucleosides when it is 2-DEOXYRIBOSE. Common Ribonucleosides ribonucleosides include CYTIDINE, URIDINE, ADENOSINE, GUANOSINE and INOSINE. Examples of Deoxy-Ribonucleosides 2'-deoxyribonucleosides include THYMIDINE and DEOXYADENOSINE. When nucleosides are phosphorylated on the sugar's primary alcohol group, the resulting phosphonucleosides are called Nucleotides nucleotides.

name: an anthracenone
annotation: An anthracenone is a molecule with an anthracene skeleton that is substituted by an oxo group.

name: a 3-(3-hydroxyalkanoyloxy)alkanoate
annotation: This class contains molecules that are composed of two 3-hydroxy fatty acid chains of varying length (represented here in part by R1 and R2) linked to each other through an ester bond between the β-hydroxyl group of one chain and the carboxyl group of another chain. They have been found to be constituents of rhamnolipids (see Mono-Rhamnolipids and a Di-Rhamnolipids) .

name: a cutin
annotation: The cuticle is one of four hydrophobic macromolecular assemblies (along with Suberins suberin, Lignins lignin and Sporopollenins sporopollenin) that enabled the terrestrialization of vascular plants. The cuticle covers all of the aerial surfaces of plants and fulfills multiple functions, e.g., control of water and gas exchanges, defense signaling against biotic and abiotic stresses, protection against UV radiation, retention of environmental pollutants, and induction of responses to mechanical stimuli . The main component of the cuticle is cutin, a waxy polymer that consists of OMEGA-HYDROXY-FATTY-ACID "ω-hydroxy fatty acids" and their derivatives, interlinked via ester bonds, and also contains cuticular waxes. It is insoluble and provides awaterproof layer. There are two major families of cutin monomers, comprising C16 and C18 compounds. The C16 family consists mainly of 16-HYDROXYPALMITATE and CPD-17653 and 1016-DIHYDROXYPALMITATE. The C18 family consists mainly of 18-HYDROXYOLEATE, 910-EPOXY-18-HYDROXYSTEARATE, and 91018-TRIHYDROXYSTEARATE. The structure shown here provides an example of a cutin segment generated by several 1016-DIHYDROXYPALMITATE molecules. In addition to cutin the cuticle also contains waxes, polysaccharides and phenolic compound. The cuticle of some plants also contains a polymeric carbohydrate known as Cutan cutan.

name: a [heparan-sulfate]-containing 2-O-sulfo-α-L-iduronate
annotation: Heparan-Sulfate "Heparan sulfate" consists of a variably sulfated repeating disaccharide unit which is composed of a hexuronic acid and a hexosamine. The most common repeating unit, which makes up around 50% of the total disaccharide units in heparan sulfate, is composed of CPD-12521 linked to CPD-12541. This compound describes CPD-12518 residues within the context of Heparan-Sulfate.
family_key: sm:name:a heparan sulfate containing 2 o sulfo l iduronate

name: amphetamine
annotation: Amphetamine is a potent central nervous system (CNS) stimulant that is used in the treatment of attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), narcolepsy, and obesity. It exists as two enantiomers: CPD-7659 and CPD-7658.. Amphetamine is not a natural product. It was first synthesized in 1887 under the name phenylisopropylamine, but at the time its effect on the nervous system was not known. It was independently resynthesized in 1927, at which time its sympathomimetic properties were reported. Most of the currently prescribed amphetamine formulations contain both enantiomers.

name: a Lewis x epitope
aliases: a 1,4-β-D-galactosyl-(α-1,3-L-fucosyl)-N-acetyl-D-glucosaminyl-[glycoprotein]|a 1,4-β-D-galactosyl-(α-1,3-L-fucosyl)-N-acetyl-D-glucosaminyl-R|a β-D-galactosyl-(1→4)-[α-L-fucosyl-(1→3)]-N-acetyl-D-glucosaminyl-R|a histo-blood group Lex antigen|SSEA-1|stage-specific embryonic antigen 1
annotation: The CPD-8594 "Lewis x" epitope, also known as stage-specific embryonic antigen-1 (SSEA-1) or CD15 , is a carbohydrate structure expressed on several glycolipids, glycoproteins, and proteoglycans of the nervous system and has been implicated in cell-cell recognition, neurite outgrowth, and neuronal migration during development. It appears to be involved in regulating neural stem cells via the Notch signaling pathway . The CPD-8594 "Lewis X epitope" is formed by α(1,3) fucosylation of the N-acetylglucosaminyl residue of B-Gal-14-NacGlc-R, the disaccharide precursor that can also lead to formation of the Type-2-H-Antigen by α(1,2) fucosylation at its terminal galactosyl residue. In mice the α-(1,3)-fucosyltransferase encoded by the HS10520 gene is the main contributor to the production of the CPD-8594 "Lewis x" epitope . The related Lewis-a-epitope is formed by α(1,4) fucosylation of the N-acetylglucosaminyl residue of beta-Gal-13-beta-GlcNac-R, which can also lead to formation of Type-1-H-Antigen by α(1,2) fucosylation at its terminal galactosyl residue. Both the CPD-8594 and the Lewis-a-epitope cannot be further transformed to ABH-type antigens . The carbohydrate moiety of the Lewis x epitope is known as CPD-16475 CD15 (cluster of differentiation 15 antigen).

name: a cobamide
annotation: Cobamides "A cobamide" is a corrinoid molecule based on the structure of CPD-20843, a cobalt-corrinoid hexaamide that is formed from COBINAMIDE by the attachment of an α-D-ribofuranose 3-phosphate residue at the 2 position of the aminopropanol chain. Cobamides serve as cofactors for a number of important enzymes. A key structural feature of cobinamides is a corrin ring with a cobalt ion in its center. Four of the six coordination sites of the cobalt ion are provided by the corrin ring, while the other two are provided by Cobamide-Upper-Ligands upper (β) and Cobamide-Lower-Ligands lower (α) ligands, which are located above and below the ring plane, respectively. The best known cobamide, Cobalamins cobalamin, contains DIMETHYLBENZIMIDAZOLE as the lower ligand and either a methyl group or 5'-deoxyadenosyl group as the upper ligand. At least 16 different cobamides with structural variability in the lower ligand have been described. The 16 bases reported so far are BENZIMIDAZOLE, CPD-18320, CPD-18321, CPD-18497, CPD-18495, DIMETHYLBENZIMIDAZOLE, ADENINE, CPD-18323, GUANINE, HYPOXANTHINE, PHENOL, CPD-108, CPD-5723, CPD-20865, CPD-20864, and CPD-20885 . Different cobamides may not be functionally equivalent as cofactors for cobamide-dependent enzymes .

name: a TAG(18:3/38:0)
annotation: This class of triacylglycerols contains three acyl chains formed by the esterification of glycerol with three fatty acids. The names of triacylglycerol compound classes provide information about the three acyl chains of each member of the class, using the nomenclature TAG(a:b/y:z) where "a" represents the number of carbons found in one acyl chain and "b" represents the number of desaturations present in chain "a" and where "y" represents the total number of carbons found in the other two acyl chains and "z" represents the total number of desaturations present in the two chains included in "y." Therefore, in this TAG(18:3/38:0) class of compounds, one acyl chain has 18 carbons and three double bonds. The other two acyl chains have a total of 38 carbons and zero double bonds. The attachment sites of the acyl chains at the R1 (sn-1), R2 (sn-2), or R3 (sn-3) position along the glyceryl backbone may vary among class members and cannot be inferred from their order in the compound class name. Additionally, the location and orientation of the double bonds in the unsaturated acyl chain(s) may vary among members of the class and are not specified by this notation.

name: an alcohol
annotation: Alcohols Alcohols are organic compounds in which the hydroxyl functional group (-OH) is bound to a carbon atom. Alcohols are classified into primary, secondary, and tertiary, based upon the number of carbon atoms connected to the carbon atom that bears the hydroxyl group. The Primary-Alcohols "primary alcohols" have general formulas RCH2OH; Secondary-Alcohols "secondary alcohols" are RR'CHOH; and Tertiary-Alcohols "tertiary alcohols" are RR'R"COH, where R, R', and R" stand for alkyl groups. Alcohols are also classified based on the length of the alkyl groups. Short-Chain-Alcohols "Short-chain alcohols" have alkyl chains with fewer than six carbons. Medium-Chain-Alcohols "Medium-chain alcohols" have alkyl chains of 6-12 carbons. Long-chain-alcohols "Long-chain alcohols" (also known as fatty alcohols) have alkyl chains of 13-22 carbons. Very-Long-Chain-Alcohols "Very long-chain alcohols" have alkyl chains of 23 carbons or longer.

annotation: A leucoanthocyanidin is Flavanols with hydroxy groups at positions 3 and 4.

name: an oxylipin
annotation: An oxylipin is any member of the group of bioactive lipids made by oxidation of polyunsaturated fatty acids.

name: a plasmalogen
annotation: Plasmalogens are a type of a glycerophospholipid characterized by the presence of a vinyl ether linkage at the sn-1 position and an ester linkage at the sn-2 position. They are found in animals and some anaerobic bacteria, but not in plants, fungi, or most aerobic bacteria, except myxobacteria. In mammals, the sn-1 position is typically derived from C16:0, C18:0, or C18:1 fatty alcohols while the sn-2 position is most commonly occupied by polyunsaturated fatty acids. When the group at the sn-1 position is saturated, the compound is called a plasmanyl-phospholipid. When the compound is unsaturated, it is known as plasmenyl-phospholipid, or plasmalogen. The most common head groups present in mammalian plasmalogens are ethanolamine (designated Alkyl-enyl-acyl-gly-P-EtOH-amines plasmenylethalomines) or choline (designated PLASMENYLCHOLINE plasmenylcholines).

name: a 2-O-α-D-galactopyranosyl-4-O-methyl-α-D-glucurono)-D-xylan
annotation: Glucuronoxylans Glucuronoxylans are a component of Hemicelluloses. They are linear polymers of β-D-xylopyranosyl units linked by (1→4) glycosidic bonds, with many of the xylose units substituted with 2,2 or 2,3-linked glucuronate residue, which are often methylated at position 4. Most of the glucuronoxylans have single 4-O-methyl-α-D-glucopyranosyl uronate residues (MeGlcA) attached at position 2. This structural type is usually named as 4-O-methyl-D-glucurono-D-xylan (MGX). An unusual heteroxylan in which some of the glucurunosyl residues are further substituted at position O-2 by ALPHA-D-GALACTOSE has been reported from the wood of TAX-34317 .

name: limonene
annotation: The two enantiomers of Limonenes "limonene" are the most-abundant monocyclic monoterpenes in nature. CPD-4886 (L-limonene) is found in a variety of trees and herbs such as Mentha spp., while CPD-8785 (D-limonene) is the major component of peel oil from oranges and lemons, and the essential oil of caraway. The natural functions of limonene are thought to include prevention of dehydration and inhibition of microbial (especially fungal) growth .

Chondroitin sulfate is composed of repeating disaccharide units composed of a hexuronic acid and a hexose sugar, specifically CPD-12521 and CPD-12557 linked by β(1 -> 3) linkages. Both units are often sulfated. This compound stand for CPD-12557 sulfated at both the 4' and 6' positions within the context of Chondroitin-sulfates.

Neoterins also contain a 1,2,3-trihydroxypropyl side chain at position 6.

name: jadomycin B
annotation: The jadomycins are a unique family of angucycline-derived antibiotics . Jadomycin B is produced by the organism TAX-953739 under stress conditions such as heat shock, phage infection, and particularly ethanol treatment . This compound is bioactive against Gram-positive (including the methicillin-resistant TAX-1280) and Gram-negative bacteria, and against yeast . Its structure includes the deoxysugar CPD-11914 . Jadomycin B exists as a diastereomeric mixture of CPD-12934 and CPD-21697 , with the former being the predominant form at a ratio of 3:2. The two forms can interconvert via an aldehyde intermediate .

name: microcin C
annotation: Microcin C, originally named microcin 7 and often referred to as microcin C7, is a heptapeptide produced by TAX-562 with an N-formylated methionine and a C-terminal phosphoramidate linkage to adenosine, modified with an aminopropyl moiety . Microcin C is exported from producing cells through a major facilitator superfamily efflux pump encoded by the G-124166 gene. It is imported by target cells via the ABC-41-CPLX "inner membrane transporter YejABEF", which recognizes the peptide part of the microcin . Following import, the peptide is cleaved by non-specific peptidases, releasing CPD-25930, a toxic CPD0-915 mimic that targets EC-6.1.1.12, inhibiting protein synthesis .

name: furfural
annotation: A heterocyclic aldehyde derived from agricultural byproducts such as wheat bran and saw dust. Also produced during the hydrolysis of pentose polymers in hemicellulose . Furfural is a toxic compound to many microorganisms, which have an array of oxidoreductases that can reduce furfural to a less toxic furfural alcohol.

name: progesterone
annotation: Progesterone is a C-21 steroid hormone involved in the female menstrual cycle, pregnancy and embryogenesis of humans and other species. Progesterone is the major naturally occurring human progestogen. In animals, progesterone is produced in the ovaries (by the corpus luteum), the adrenal glands, and, during pregnancy, in the placenta. In addition, progesterone is also produced by some plant species, such as TAX-51240 .

name: γ-glutamyl-ethylamide
annotation: Theanine is a unique amino acid that renders the sweet flavor of Japanese tea. In addition to its flavor-enhancing role, theanine was shown to have favorable physiological functions towards several animals and humans .

name: arsenite2-
annotation: Arsenic is the most prevalent environmental toxic substance. It ranks first on the priority list of Hazardous Substances published by the Agency for Toxic Substances and Disease Registry (ATSDR), based on a combination of frequency, toxicity, and potential for human exposure. It is also a Group 1 human carcinogen associated with skin, bladder and lung cancer. While arsenic exists in two oxidation states, AsIII and AsV, in aerobic environments most inorganic arsenic is found as As(V), such as ARSENATE. In anaerobic environments the prevalent form is As(III), such as CPD0-2040. While most of the literature refers to the As(III) form found in the environment as CPD-763, at natural pH values most of the AsIII molecules exist in solution as CPD0-2040, an inorganic polyol similar to glycerol, since the pKa values are high: pKa1 = 9:2 and pKa2 = 12:7 . This form of arsenite can enter cells via glycerol channels such as EG10396 GlpF of TAX-562 or aquaglyceroporins, bidirectional channels that allow water and small uncharged molecules such as glycerol to pass through. Charged forms of arsenite, formed at higher pH, include CPD-763 "arsenite1-", CPD-24319 and CPD-24318.

name: erythromycin A
annotation: CPD-13804 "Erythromycin A" is a macrolide antibiotic with an antimicrobial spectrum similar to or slightly wider than that of penicillin. The antibiotic was isolated in 1952 from TAX-1836 by scientists from of the Lilly research laboratories . It was given the trade name "Ilotycin", but the name erythromycin was introduced the same year in an editorial announcing this antibiotic Erythromycin A is used in clinical medicine against infections caused by Gram-positive bacteria. It is also used for many pulmonary infections such as Legionnaire's disease and as an alternative for patients allergic to penicillins . Erythromycin contains a 14-membered lactone ring with ten asymmetric centers (the aglycone is called CPD-13947) and two sugars (CPD-13969 and CPD-13780). The structure was elucidated in 1957 , and the absolute stereochemistry was assigned by X-ray crystallography in 1965 . Erythromycin binds to the 50s subunit of the bacterial 70s rRNA complex, inhibiting protein synthesis, and thus stopping structural and functional processes that are critical for life or replication. More specifically, erythromycin interferes with aminoacyl translocation, preventing the transfer of the tRNA bound at the A site of the rRNA complex to the P site of the rRNA complex.

name: cyclobis-(1→6)-α-nigerosyl
annotation: CPD-19461 "Cyclobis-(1→6)-α-nigerosyl" is a cyclic glucotetraose with alternate α(1→6)- and α(1→3)-glucosidic linkages, produced by several bacterial species during the degradation of complex glycans. The compound was first described from TAX-1245, in which it was formed by the hydrolysis of Alternans alternan . It was later reported that in TAX-1459 it is synthesized from MALTOTETRAOSE . Similar enzymes were reported from a Bacillus sp. and an TAX-1665 strain . Following its import into the cell, the compound is degraded, serving as a carbon source .

name: fosfomycin
annotation: Fosfomycin is a broad-spectrum antibiotic produced by certain Streptomyces species. There are several different stereochemical representations of this compound in the literature . The one shown here is according to . Fosfomycin is a mimetic of PHOSPHO-ENOL-PYRUVATE, and irreversibly binds to the catalytic cysteine residue of EC-2.5.1.7 (G-11844), blocking peptidoglycan biosynthesis in Gram-negative bacteria.

name: N,N'-dicyclohexylcarbodiimide
annotation: CPD-10322 (DCCD) is a classical inhibitor of ATP synthase by binding to one of the c subunits and causing steric hindrance of the rotation of the FO subcomplex .

name: indolmycin
annotation: CPD0-920 Indolmycin is an antibacterial drug produced by both terrestrial and marine bacteria that exhibits potent antibacterial activity against human pathogens such as TAX-210 and mupirocin-, methicillin-resistant TAX-1280 . Indolmycin, discovered in 1960 , is a structural analog of TRP and competitively inhibits the bacterial EC-6.1.1.2 . Organisms that produce indolmycin are resistant to it due to the presence of a resistant tryptophan--tRNA ligase . Indolmycin has not been tested in humans, although it has been proposed to be tested as a topical treatment for staphylococcal infections. The indolmycin structure contains an oxazolinone ring in its core, making it of interest to synthetic chemists, and over the years several total synthetic routes have been reported . The complete biosynthetic pathway has been described in 2015 .

name: cortisol
annotation: Cortisol is a glucocorticoid steroid hormone, produced by the zona fasciculata of the adrenal cortex. It is the main glucocorticoid hormone in humans, and its primary functions are to increase blood sugar through gluconeogenesis; suppress the immune system; and aid in fat, protein and carbohydrate metabolism.

name: 2-heptyl-4-hydroxyquinoline N-oxide
annotation: CPD-7975 "2-Heptyl-4-hydroxyquinoline N-oxide" (HQNO) is a respiratory toxin produced by TAX-287 that suppresses the growth of many Gram-positive bacteria . HQNO is potent inhibitor of respiratory electron transfer . It binds to the quinone reduction site of the cytochrome bc1 complex and inhibits quinol oxidase activity of cytochrome bo3, bd, and type II NADH-quinone oxidoreductase .

name: rapamycin
annotation: The macrocyclic polyketides CPD-10016, CPD-18261 (FK520), and CPD-14676, which are produced by TAX-83656, TAX-132248, and TAX-1912, respectively, are potent immunosuppressants that prevent T-cell proliferation through initial binding to the immunophilin FKBP12. Analogs of these molecules are of considerable interest as therapeutics in both metastatic and inflammatory disease.

name: sebacate
aliases: sebacic acid|1,10-decanedioic acid|1,8-Octanedicarboxylic acid|n-decanedioic acid|decanedioic acid|decanedicarboxylic acid
annotation: CPD-3623 Sebacate (sebacic acid) is a naturally occurring dicarboxylic acid. It is found in the sebum (the secretion of sebaceous glands) and other lipids that coat the skin surface. Sebacate is used commercially as a monomer for nylon 610 and in the production of plasticizers, lubricants, hydraulic fluids, cosmetics, candles, and other products. Sebacate is one of the main three ingredients in royal jelly, and was shown to have a potent antileishmanial effect .

name: cerulenin
annotation: Cerulenin is a fungal toxin that inhibits both eukaryotic and prokaryotic ketoacyl-acyl carrier protein synthases or condensing enzymes. It has been used experimentally to treat cancer and obesity, and is a potent inhibitor of bacterial growth .

name: valinomycin
annotation: CPD-20899 Valinomycin is a cyclooligomer Depsipeptides depsipeptide produced by several Streptomyces strains . It has diverse biological activities including antifungal , antibacterial , insecticidal-nematocidal , antiparasitic , antiviral , and antitumor activities. Of particular interest is that valinomycin demonstrated a high potency against SARS-CoV in infected Vero E6 cells with an EC50 of 0.85 μm . CPD-20899 Valinomycin forms an ionophore that specifically modulates potassium ion transport across biological membranes, which may explain some of its biological activity . CPD-20899 Valinomycin is a dodecadepsipeptide assembled from 3 identical copies of the tetradepsipeptide CPD-25315, which contains alternating amide and ester bonds . Interestingly, a very similar compound produced by TAX-1892, CPD-25325, is a cyclic octadepsipeptide consisting of two copies of the same tetradepsipeptide . The biosynthesis of CPD-20899 is described in PWY-8336.

name: fusidic acid
annotation: Fusidate is a steroidal antibacterial agent that is used topically or systemically, usually against infections caused by Gram-positive pathogens.

name: homotaurine
annotation: Homotaurine is a natural product of marine red algae. It is a component of some pharmaceuticals, and is also used as an over-the-counter 'memory enhancer' .

name: ferulate
annotation: FERULIC-ACID "Ferulate" was first described in 1866 in the green plant TAX-371345. It is an abundant and nearly ubiquitous phenolic CPD-674 "cinnamate" derivative synthesized in plants. FERULIC-ACID "Ferulate" is rarely found in free form within plant material - it occurs linked to various carbohydrates as glycosidic conjugates, and as various esters and amides within a wide variety of natural products, such as lignin .

name: triclosan
annotation: Triclosan is an antibacterial and antifungal agent. At lower concentrations triclosan appears bacteriostatic and targets bacteria mainly by inhibiting fatty acid synthesis via its binding to the bacterial ENOYL-ACP-REDUCT-NADH-MONOMER, which is encoded by EG11528. Triclosan forms a tight, non-covalent ternary complex with the oxidized NADH cofactor in the active site of the enzyme . Some Gram-positive bacteria are naturally resistant to triclosan due to the presence of triclosan-resistant enoyl-ACP reductase isoforms, encoded by G-15302 . In addition, some Gram-negative bacteria have a triclosan-resistant isoform encoded by G-15301 . Some Gram-positive organisms have a different isoform encoded by BSU08650. FabL is inhibited by triclosan in a reversible manner, but does not form a stable NADtriclosan ternary complex . At higher concentration triclosan acts as a biocide, with multiple cytoplasmic and membrane targets .

name: (S)-propane-1,2-diol
annotation: 1,2-Propanediol (1,2-PDO) occurs in two stereoisomers - PROPANE-1-2-DIOL and CPD-8891. Both forms are produced by certain organisms. For example, during the catabolism of 6-deoxyhexoses or during the fermentation of glucose via methylglyoxal . 1,2-PDO is a bulk industrial chemical with a global demand of around 1.36 million tons/year . It is widely used in the production of unsaturated polyester resin and as a nontoxic replacement of ethylene glycol in deicer and antifreeze products. Current commercial production of 1,2-PDO is mainly by a synthetic process that results in a racemic mixture.

name: menadione
annotation: Vitamin K is a name given to a group of derivatives of 2-methyl-1,4-naphthoquinone that are required by mammals for the posttranslational modification of certain proteins, mostly involved in blood coagulation. These compounds, while essential for mammals, are not synthesized by them, and are thus considered vitamins. The natural forms of the vitamin are vitamin K1 (2-METHYL-3-PHYTYL-14-NAPHTHOQUINONE) and vitamin K2 (Menaquinones). Since menaquinones produced by different organisms have different tail lenghts, there are many variations of vitamin K2, usually specified based on the number of carbons in their tails, such as in Vitamin K2(45). Menaquinones are synthesized by gut bacteria, and are absorbed into the blood. Thus, dietary deficiency is extremely rare in healthy individuals. CPD-3766 Menadione, also known as vitamin K3, is the most active synthetic form of the vitamin, which lacks the isoprenoid tail.

name: pentaerythritol tetranitrate
annotation: PETN is a vasodilator with properties similar to those of CPD-143, but with a more prolonged duration of action, and is used for treatment of angina pectoris. It is also one of the most powerful high explosives known and is a component of the plastic explosive known as Semtex.

annotation: This is a degradation product of the dye CPD-12792, produced by combined oxidative and hydrolytic steps catalyzed by the dye decolorizing peroxidase (EC 1.11.1.19 ) .

name: iron(III)-coprogen
annotation: Coprogen is a hydroxamate compound isolated from fungi and bacteria, including Penicillium species and Neurospora crassa. It is a growth factor for coprophilous fungi.

name: 1H-imidazole
annotation: The imidazole ring is aromatic due to the presence of a sextet of π-electrons, consisting of a pair of electrons from the protonated nitrogen atom and one from each of the remaining four atoms of the ring. Thus the compound exists in several resonance structures. Imidazole also exists in two equivalent tautomeric forms, IMIDAZOLE-RING and CPD-19703. Different numbering systems have been used leading to confusion in the naming of imidazole compounds. When a side chain exists (as in L-histidine), the nitrogen atoms of the ring are denoted by pros ('near', abbreviated π) and tele ('far', abbreviated T) to show their position relative to the side chain. When a substituent is introduced at positions 4 or 5, the tautomeric character of the molecule precludes a definite numbering of these positions, resulting in names in the form "4(or 5)-substituted imidazole", as in CPD-54 "4(or 5)-aminoimidazole". However, when the imino hydrogen is substituted, tautomerism is no longer possible, and the numbering of positions 4 and 5 becomes absolute, as in CPD-19704.

name: ethyl acetate
annotation: ETHYLACETATE "Ethyl acetate" is one of the most important small volatile esters. It is used as a chemical solvent and is used in the synthesis of biodiesels, paints, adhesives, herbicides and resins . Its annual production volume was estimated at 3.5 million tonnes in 2015, which corresponds to a $3.7 billion global market.

name: teicoplanin
annotation: Teicoplanin is an antibiotic used in the prophylaxis and treatment of serious infections caused by Gram-positive bacteria, including methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus and Enterococcus faecalis. It has a spectrum of activity similar to CPD-12245. Its mechanism of action is to inhibit bacterial cell wall synthesis . Teicoplanin is a complex mixture of related natural products isolated from the fermentation broth of a strain of Actinoplanes teichomyceticus. The mixture includes five major components (CPD-26433, CPD-26434, CPD-26436, CPD-26437, and CPD-26438), a hydrolysis component (CPD-26439), and four minor components (teicoplanin RS-1 through RS-4).

name: microcin B17 precursor peptide containing azole residues
annotation: Microcin B17 is a linear azol(in)e-containing peptide (LAP) that functions as a narrow-spectrum bactericidal peptide. The peptide targets DNA gyrase . It is active mostly against Enterobacteriaceae and some Pseudomonas species, due to its limited ability to enter bacterial cells. The precursor peptide is encoded by the mcbA gene. All four CYS and four of the six SER residues are modified by EC-6.2.2.2 to azoline residues, which are further oxidized to azole rings. These modifications are catalyzed by a trimeric synthetase comprising McbB, McbC, and McbD (substrate recognition module, dehydrogenase, and YcaO proteins, respectively). Following generation of the azole rings the leader region is removed by a protease (tldD/tldE) encoded on the chromosome of E. coli. After complete processing, a two-component ABC transporter (McbE/McbF) exports the mature microcin B17 while also providing a level of self-protection. Microcin B17 is imported into target cells by two membrane proteins - the outer membrane protein OmpF and the plasma membrane protein SmbA. Once inside the cells, the peptide inhibits DNA gyrase and triggers double strand breaks in DNA.

name: platensimycin
annotation: CPD-15302 Platensimycin (PTM) is a potent and selective inhibitor of the elongation-specific condensing enzymes of type II fatty acid biosynthesis (EC-2.3.1.41 (FabB) and EC-2.3.1.179 (FabF)). The compound, produced by TAX-58346, was discovered in 2006 by researchers at Merck, using an antisense differential sensitivity screening focused on selective inhibitors of fatty acid biosynthesis . PTM interacts specifically with the acyl-enzyme intermediate of the FabF enzyme. Crystalographic studies have shown that it binds in the malonyl subsite of FabF, with its benzoic acid ring positioned to interact with important residues of the catalytic machinery. Because of its unique mode of action, platensimycin is efficient against notorious antibiotic-resistant strains such as methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA), vancomycin-intermediate S. aureus, and vancomycin-resistant enterococci. PTM is not active against wild-type TAX-562 due to efflux mechanisms, has no antifungal activity, and has low mammalian cell toxicity . The utility of CPD-15302 as a drug is hampered by poor pharmacokinetic properties and negligible oral bioavailability. Clinical trials have been delayed pending the development of chemical analogues which have more favorable drug-like properties .

annotation: It is a very toxic and complex pyrone derivatives produced by the fungus TAX-240499.

name: tetracycline
annotation: The tetracyclines are a subclass of polyketides that have an octahydrotetracene-2-carboxamide skeleton, substituted with hydroxy and other groups. Many of them are potent antibiotics. The compound CPD0-1414 is produced naturally in small amount by TAX-1894, whose major tetracycline product is CPD-19258, the first tetracycline compound to be discovered . Upon addition of bromide (which inhibits the chlorination) to the culture broth, the organism produces up to 99% CPD0-1414 .

name: 2,6-diaminopurine
annotation: Back in 1977 it was discovered that the genome of TAX-260586 does not contain ADENINE bases. Instead, the phage genome contains the nucleotide base CPD0-2060 (Z) . CPD0-2060 2,6-Diaminopurine forms three hydrogen bonds when base-paired with THYMINE witihin the double helix DNA structure, changing the physical, chemical, and mechanical properties of the double stranded DNA . As a result, the genomic DNA of TAX-260586 is resistant to all restriction enzymes whose recognition sequence involves adenine . Interestingly, the Z base was identified in a carbonaceous meteorite and was proposed as a nucleobase that could have been available at the time life evolved on earth .

name: tellurite
annotation: Tellurite is the most abundant species of tellurium, a chalcogenic element related to selenium. Like other oxyanion derivatives of tellurium and selenium tellurite is toxic to living organisms even at very low levels . Bacteria have developed mechanisms to overcome its toxicity by either reducing it to elemental tellurium, which is insoluble in water and almost non-toxic , or by methylating it .

name: Brij 97
annotation: Brij is a registered trademark of ICI Americas, Inc, used to name a series of detergents that are ethers of polyoxyethylene with long-chain alkanes. In general, increasing the alkyl group length decreasees water solubility, while increasing the POE polymer length increases water solubility. Brij 97 is an ether of POE of about 10 oxyethylene units with an oleoyl group. It is often used for protein solubilization in biochemical applications.

name: aspirin
aliases: salicylic acid acetate|o-carboxyphenyl acetate|O-acetylsalicylic acid|o-acetoxybenzoic acid|Easprin|Azetylsalizylsaeure|ASA|acidum acetylsalicylicum|acido acetilsalicilico|acide acetylsalicylique|acide 2-(acetyloxy)benzoique|Acetylsalicylsaeure|2-acetoxybenzoic acid|acetylsalicylic acid|2-acetoxybenzenecarboxylic acid|acetylsalicylate
annotation: Extracts from bark of the willow tree (TAX-3688) and from TAX-23224 shrubs had been known to help alleviate headaches, pains, and fevers since antiquity. The natural active ingredient in these extracts is the plant hormone CPD-110. Unfortunately, while effective, salicylate has an undesired side effects, as it is known to upset the stomach. In 1897, chemists working at Bayer AG produced a synthetically altered version of CPD-1142, derived from the species TAX-57917 (meadowsweet), which caused less digestive upset than pure CPD-110. The new drug, acetylsalicylate, was named Aspirin, which is derived from "acetyl" and "Spirsaure", an old German name for salicylate derived from the genus name TAX-23224. CPD-524 Aspirin is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) used for pain relief, fever reduction, and against swelling. Like other NSAID drugs (e.g. CPD-7669 and Ibuprofen), aspirin works by inhibiting the enzyme EC-1.14.99.1 (also known as cyclooxygenase or COX) that converts ARACHIDONIC_ACID to PROSTAGLANDIN-H2, a precursor to other prostaglandins that mediate pain, inflammation, and fever. In addition, PROSTAGLANDIN-H2 is also a precursor of ALPHA11-ALPHA-EPOXY-15-HYDROXYTHROMBA, which stimulates platelet aggregation. The inhibition of the COX1 variant of the COX cyclooxygenase also reduces the formation of blood clots. Aspirin acts by acetylating a serine residue in the active site of the COX cyclooxygenase. Aspirin is much more active on the HS01815 (COX1) variant than on the HS01115 (COX2) variant. The popularity of aspirin grew over the first half of the 20th century, largely due to its effectiveness during the Spanish flu pandemic of 1918, but declined after CPD-7669 was released in 1956, followed by Ibuprofen in 1969. name: L-carnitine
annotation: CARNITINE is a zwitterionic, quaternary amine carboxylate that has essential and diverse roles in intermediary metabolism in all living kingdoms. Carnitine was first discovered in 1905 in muscle extracts, and the name is derived from the Latin word for flesh, Carnis . In 1962 it was found that the physiological enantiomer is CARNITINE, and since then the widespread distribution of L-carnitine has been established in higher and lower eukaryotes, plants, and many microorganisms . In animals and yeast carnitine is best known for its contribution to fatty acid intracellular trafficking . In plants L-carnitine is also associated with fatty acid metabolism, although its exact function is less well understood . Bacteria are not able to synthesize carnitine do novo, but are able to import the compound and use it as an osmoprotectant or to enhance thermotolerance, cryotolerance and barotolerance . CARNITINE is used in pharmaceutical and nutritional preparations and methods have been described for its production and separation from its D-CARNITINE enantiomer and reviewed in .

name: fosmidomycin
annotation: Fosmidomycin, an antibiotic isolated from TAX-1914, is a potent inhibitor of EC-1.1.1.267, a key enzyme of the NONMEVIPP-PWY "methylerythritol phosphate pathway", an alternative pathway of isoprenoid synthesis. Many Eubacteria, as well as the malaria parasite TAX-5820, are dependent on this pathway, because they lack the mevalonate-based pathway.

name: polymyxin M
annotation: This antibiotic is produced by ORG-6251. It is similar to the structure of CPD-16444 except that CPD-16461 contains a L-diaminobutanoyl residue at position 3 rather than a D-diaminobutanoyl residue .

name: microcin J25
annotation: Microcin J25 is a plasmid-encoded antimicrobial peptide produced by some strains of Escherichia coli. It consists of 21 amino acids which form an unusual fold known as a 'lassoed tail' in which the C-terminal tail of the peptide is trapped within an 8 residue 'lariat' ring .

name: tacrolimus
annotation: The macrocyclic polyketides CPD-10016 (FK506), CPD-18261 (FK520), and CPD-14676 are potent immunosuppressants that prevent T-cell proliferation through initial binding to the immunophilin FKBP12. Analogs of these molecules are of considerable interest as therapeutics in both metastatic and inflammatory disease. CPD-10016 Tacrolimus and CPD-18261 bind to the receptor macrophilin-12 (FKBP-12) forming a complex that blocks the calcium-dependent signal transduction cascade mediated by calcineurin. Calcineurin is a phsphatase that is responsible for activating nuclear factor of activated T-cells (NF-AT), a T cell transcriptional regulatory factor. As a consequence of the macrolide-FKBP-12 complex formation, the synthesis and release of Th1- (T helper 1) and Th2- (T helper 2) type cytokines, and other inflammatory mediators from T-cells and mast cells are blocked and the expression of signals essential for the activation of inflammatory T-lymphocytes is inhibited.

name: 3'-azido-3'-deoxythymidine
annotation: CPD-15110 is a nucleoside analog reverse-transcriptase inhibitor (NRTI), an antiretroviral drug used for the treatment of HIV/AIDS infectiousness.

name: decoyinine
annotation: Decoyinine is purine nucleoside analogue that blocks the conversion of xanthosine monophosphate to guanosine monophosphate .

name: arsenite
annotation: Arsenic is the most prevalent environmental toxic substance. It ranks first on the priority list of Hazardous Substances published by the Agency for Toxic Substances and Disease Registry (ATSDR), based on a combination of frequency, toxicity, and potential for human exposure. It is also a Group 1 human carcinogen associated with skin, bladder and lung cancer. While arsenic exists in two oxidation states, AsIII and AsV, in aerobic environments most inorganic arsenic is found as As(V), such as ARSENATE. In anaerobic environments the prevalent form is As(III), such as CPD0-2040. While most of the literature refers to the As(III) form found in the environment as CPD-763, at natural pH values most of the AsIII molecules exist in solution as CPD0-2040, an inorganic polyol similar to glycerol, since the pKa values are high: pKa1 = 9:2 and pKa2 = 12:7 . This form of arsenite can enter cells via glycerol channels such as EG10396 GlpF of TAX-562 or aquaglyceroporins, bidirectional channels that allow water and small uncharged molecules such as glycerol to pass through. Charged forms of arsenite, formed at higher pH, include CPD-763 "arsenite1-", CPD-24319 and CPD-24318.

name: aldehydo 3,6-anhydro-α-L-galactose
annotation: CPD-13430 "3,6-Anhydro-α-L-galactopyranose" is the major monomeric sugar component of the glycans found in red macroalgae, such as Agaroses agarose and Porphyrans porphyran.

name: chloramphenicol
annotation: Chloramphenicol is a bacteriostatic antibiotic that inhibits protein synthesis by binding to the 50S subunit of the bacterial ribosome. The antibiotic binds to the A-site of the 50S subunit and occupies the binding site for the amino-acyl moiety of the A-site tRNA . It is active against both Gram-negative and Gram-positive bacteria, including many multiply drug-resistant strains. Chloramphenicol's ability to cross the blood-brain barrier makes it a potent therapeutic against brain infections. Chloramphenicol is produced by several Gram-positive soil actinomycetes, but its biosynthesis has been analyzed mostly in TAX-953739 .

name: glyphosate
annotation: Glyphosate is a broad-spectrum systemic herbicide used to kill weeds. It was initially patented and sold by Monsanto Company in the 1970s under the tradename Roundup, and has become the most used herbicide in the world . Glyphosate's mode of action is to inhibit the enzyme EC-2.5.1.19, which catalyzes the transfer of the enolpyruvyl moiety of PHOSPHO-ENOL-PYRUVATE to SHIKIMATE-5P to form 3-ENOLPYRUVYL-SHIKIMATE-5P. This is a key reaction in the ARO-PWY "shikimate pathway", leading to synthesis of aromatic amino acids via CHORISMATE in plants, fungi, bacteria, and apicomplexan parasites .

name: Reactive blue 19
annotation: Remazol Brilliant Blue R (RBBR) is an anthraquinone dye used in textile industries. It is toxic and can damage aquatic life.

name: daptomycin
annotation: Daptomycin is a lipopeptide antibiotic produced by TAX-67294 that is used in the treatment of systemic and life-threatening infections caused by Gram-positive organisms.

name: ceftobiprole
annotation: Ceftobiprole is a fifth-generation cephalosporin antibiotic.

name: L-canavanine
annotation: CANAVANINE was first discovered and isolated in 1929 from jack bean (TAX-3823) . Its occurrence is limited to legumes , where it often serves as the main nitrogen storage compound in seeds, with a content of up to 12% of the seeds dry weight . CANAVANINE also serves as an antimetabolite of ARG involved in plant defense. The toxic effects of canavanine originate from the mimicry of arginine, thereby interfering with arginine metabolism and protein functions when incorporated during translation. Arginyl-tRNA synthetases are usually not able to discriminate between ARG and CANAVANINE, resulting in the incorporation of the latter into nascent polypeptide chains and in the formation of dysfunctional proteins . Upon germination CANAVANINE is degraded to L-CANALINE and UREA, which can be further degraded to HOMO-SER, CARBON-DIOXIDE and AMMONIUM . L-CANALINE forms stable oximes with 2-oxoglutarate and other carbonyl-containing compounds, causing deleterious effects in the tricarboxylic acid cycle .

name: isoniazid
annotation: ISONIAZIDE Isoniazid is a clinically approved antituberculosis agent. It was first described in 1912 and has been used as an anti-tuberculosus drug since 1952 . Its activity is specific to the mycobacteria genus . Its high bactericidal activity, low cost, high bioavailability, excellent intracellular penetration, and narrow spectrum of action have made ISONIAZIDE one of the best antimicrobial agents Isoniazid is a prodrug and must be activated within TAX-1773 by the CPLX-9239 . KatG forms CPD-20007, which spontaneously couples with NAD+/NADH to form an CPD-20008 . The precise mechanism of ISONIAZIDE action on TAX-1773 is still poorly understood . However, one well-documented primary target is EC-1.3.1.118, which is encoded by the G185E-5668 gene. The CPD-20008 binds tightly to this enzyme, thereby blocking the action of fatty acid synthase and the synthesis of mycolic acids .

name: mycophenolate
annotation: CPD-14601 Mycophenolate (MPA) is an immunosuppressant that is widely used for the prevention or treatment of acute rejection after solid organ transplantation. MMF is given orally in the form of the prodrug CPD-14603 "Mycophenolate mofetil" (MMF), which is absorbed and hydrolyzed to MPA by carboxylesterases (CES) . MPA is a reversible inhibitor of inosine monophosphate dehydrogenase in T cells and B cells. This enzyme participates in purine biosynthesis (specifically guanine synthesis), and is necessary for the growth of those cells (other cell types are able to recover purines via a separate salvage pathway and are thus not sensitive to MPA). CPD-14603 "Mycophenolate mofetil" is also used in the treatment of autoimmune diseases, such as Behcet's disease, pemphigus vulgaris, and systemic lupus erythematosus. MPA is primarily metabolized by glucuronidation to CPD-14604 and CPD-14602. The latter, which may be an immunotoxic metabolite, is deglucuronidated in human liver by RXN-13605 "mycophenolic acid acyl-glucuronide esterase".

name: 2-heptyl-1-hydroxy-4(1H)-quinolinone
annotation: CPD-23999 2-heptyl-1-hydroxy-4(1H)-quinolinone is a tautomer of CPD-7975 (HQNO), which is a respiratory toxin produced by TAX-287 that suppresses the growth of many Gram-positive bacteria . This 1H-4-oxo tautomer is presumed to prevail in aqueous solutions at pH 7 .

name: antimonous acid
annotation: The pKa of antimonite is 11.8, so in neutral solutions all antimonite is present as CPD0-2039 .

name: vanadate
annotation: Even though conventionally the vanadate is represented with a single double bond, this is a resonance form, as the ion is a regular tetrahedron with four equivalent oxygen atoms.

name: 4-deoxypyridoxine 5'-phosphate
annotation: Despite its similarity to PYRIDOXAL_PHOSPHATE (PLP), the active form of vitamin B6, CPD0-1605 cannot be used as a source of vitamin B6 biosynthesis. Rather, it interferes with vitamin B6 homeostasis by acting as an extremely strong competitive inhibitor of EC-2.7.1.35. In addition, the phosphorylated form CPD0-1221 inhibits PLP-dependent enzymes .

name: p-chloromercuribenzoate
annotation: p-Chloromercuribenzoate reacts with thiol groups in proteins and is therefore an inhibitor of enzymes that are dependent on thiol reactivity.

name: fumagillin
annotation: The mold TAX-746128 produces the meroterpenoid CPD0-1436 , which irreversibly binds to and inhibits HS03371-MONOMER (MetAP2, EC-3.4.11.18 "EC 3.4.11.18") and has been studied for its anti-angiogenic properties . CPD0-1436 Fumagillin also has pharmaceutical potential for the treatment of microsporidiosis, and is the only effective chemical treatment currently available for honey bee nosemiasis, which is caused by parasitic fungi from the TAX-6029 phylum . The compound is structurally composed of a highly oxygenated cyclohexane-containing terpenoid (CPD-26043) that is esterified with a decatetraenedioic acid. CPD-26043 Fumigallol is derived from the sesquiterpene CPD-13402, which has been isolated from TAX-746128 . The gene cluster encoding CPD0-1436 biosynthesis was identified based on the presence of a polyketide synthase gene (since the decatetraenedioic acid moirty was predicted to be produced by a reduced polyketide pathway) and two genes encoding methionine aminopeptidases, which provide a self-resistance mechanism against the effects of CPD0-1436 .

name: 2,2'-disulfonyl azobenzene
annotation: This is a degradation product of the dye CPD-12792, produced by combined oxidative and hydrolytic steps catalyzed by the dye decolorizing peroxidase (EC 1.11.1.19 ) .

name: 3,6-diamino-10-methylacridinium
annotation: An aminoacridine that has antiseptic properties. Commercial preparations of acriflavine consist of a mixture of 3,6-diamino-10-methylacridinium chloride, CPD-21022 (proflavine) and other impurities .

name: oxidized DCPIP
annotation: 2,6-dichlorophenolindophenol ( DCPIP ) is a compound that is often used as a redox dye. DCPIP "Oxidized DCPIP" is blue, DCPIPH, which is reduced by a single electron, is pink, and fully CPD-13079 "reduced DCPIP" is colorless. It is often used in measurements of the electron transport chain in plants because of its higher affinity for electrons than ferredoxin. It is also used as an artificial electron acceptor for enzyme assays.

name: clindamycin
annotation: Clindamycin is a lincosamide antibiotic used in clinical practice. It is a derivatives of CPD-13893 and is prepared semisynthetically. Clindamycin exerts its bacteriostatic function by inhibiting the peptidyltransferase reaction on the 50S ribosomal subunit and blocking protein synthesis in sensitive bacterial strains .

name: nitrocefin
annotation: A chromogenic cephalosporin that is used to assay β-lactamase activty.

name: pseudomonate A
annotation: Pseudomonic acids are polyketide antibiotics produced by ORG-6360 . The antibiotic mupirocin is a mixture of four pseudomonic acids: CPD0-2581 (about 90%), CPD-21448 (about 8%), CPD-18977 (Ile]"| and ultimately protein synthesis. The main ingredient of mupirocin, CPD0-2581, consists of a C17 polyketide-derived substructure (CPD-21453) esterified by CPD-21101 . For the biosynthesis of pseudomonic acids, see PWY-8012.

name: selenide
annotation: Selenium is an essential trace element that is crucial for the normal development of bacteria and animals. High concentrations of selenium are however toxic, largely because transporters and enzymes involved in sulfur and sulfo-compound metabolism can often erroneously substitute Elemental-selenium for sulfur and can misincorporate seleno-amino acids into proteins . In humans the safety threshold between acute selenium poisoning (>400 μg/day) and selenium deficiency (<40 μg/day) is very narrow. The natural forms of selenium include SELENATE [Se(VI)], SELENITE [Se(IV)], Elemental-selenium [Se(0)], and SE-2 [Se(-II)]. Selenite is the most toxic, while elemental selenium is insoluble and is generally considered to be non-toxic . However, selenium rarely occurs in its elemental state in nature. Elemental selenium can be formed by certain organisms, usually by reduction of the oxidized forms SELENATE and SELENITE. Some bacterial species are known to form selenium nanoparticles that consist of elemental selenium coated by a mixture of proteins, lipids, and carbohydrates .

name: N3-(4-methoxyfumaroyl)-L-2,3-diaminopropanoate
annotation: N3-(4-methoxyfumaroyl)-L-2,3-diaminopropanoate is a synthetic molecule that is able to inactivate the bacterial EC-2.6.1.16. The molecule is likely attacked by the thiolate side chain of the active site Cys in the glutaminase domain of the enzyme. The alkylation of this residue blocks generation of the ammonia, which is required to convert FRUCTOSE-6P into D-GLUCOSAMINE-6-P .

name: ellagic acid
annotation: Ellagic acid is an organic heterotetracyclic compound that is formed by the dimerization of CPD-183 by oxidative aromatic coupling that involves intramolecular lactonization of both carboxylic acid groups of the resulting biaryl. It is found in many fruits and vegetables, including raspberries, strawberries, cranberries, and pomegranates, mostly in a bound form as part of different Ellagitannins ellagitannins.

name: coprogen
annotation: Coprogen is a hydroxamate compound isolated from fungi and bacteria, including Penicillium species and Neurospora crassa. It is a growth factor for coprophilous fungi.

name: paraquat
annotation: Paraquat is the trade name for N,N'-dimethyl-4,4-bipyridinium dichloride, one of the most widely used herbicides in the world. In light-exposed plants paraquat accepts electrons from ferredoxin carrying the electrons from photosystem I and transfers them to molecular oxygen, forming destructive reactive oxygen species (mostly superoxide anions). Paraquat is also highly toxic to mammals due to its redox activity. It is easily reduced by electron donors such as NADPH, and similarly transfers the electrons to molecular oxygen, producing superoxide. It has been linked to the development of Parkinson's disease and is banned in several countries.

name: allicin
annotation: CPD-9275 Allicin is a defense molecule produced by garlic (TAX-4682 with a broad range of biological activities. Allicin is physiologically active in microbial, plant and mammalian cells, due to its ability to participate in redox-reactions with thiol groups in glutathione and proteins. In a dose-dependent manner allicin can inhibit the proliferation of both bacteria and fungi, and even kill cells, including antibiotic-resistant strains like methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) .

name: carboxymethoxylamine
annotation: CARBOXYMETHOXYLAMINE Carboxymethoxylamine, also known as aminooxyacetate, is a widely used aminotransferase inhibitor.

name: cefoperazone
annotation: Cefoperazone is a semi-synthetic parenteral cephalosporin with a tetrazolyl moiety that confers β-lactamase resistance.

name: kanamycin A
annotation: Kanamycins Kanamycins are a group of Aminoglycoside-Antibiotics "aminoglycoside antibiotics" produced by the bacterium TAX-1967. The compounds were discovered in 1957 by researchers in the National Institute of Health of Tokyo, Japan . Due to their wide range of activity, especially against severe staphylococcal and mycobacterial infections, these antibiotics gained almost immediate acceptance, and the most common form, CPD-4821, was produced commercially within a year of its discovery . Kanamycins interact with the 30S subunit of the prokaryotic ribosome, inducing substantial amounts of mistranslation and indirectly inhibiting translocation during protein synthesis, resulting in death . Kanamycins are currently used to treat a wide variety of infections. Kanamycins share several biosynthetic intermediates with other aminoglycosides, including the core moiety CPD-10151 and the pseudodisaccharides CPD-14126, CPD-14125 and CPD-14137. While the main product produced by TAX-1967 is CPD-4821, additional products are also produced, including CPD-4822, CPD-4823 , CPD-14138 and CPD-4824 .

name: citreoviridin
annotation: It is a metabolite of Penicillium citreoviride thought to be the cause of yellow rice poisoning, manifested in humans and experimental animals by respiratory and circulatory failure, paralysis, convulsions and death. It is identical to beriberi, originally considered to be a thiamin nutritional deficiency.

name: β-palatinose
annotation: CPD-20122 Palatinose, also known as isomaltulose, is a reducing disaccharide of about one-third the sweetness of sucrose, that is very resistant to hydrolysis by acid and by EC-3.2.1.26. It has very similar physical properties to sucrose, and is found naturally in small quantities in honey .

name: polymyxin B5
annotation: Polymyxin B5 is an antibiotic produced by TAX-1406 that is used in the treatment of Gram-negative bacterial infections. It differs from other polymyxin B compounds by having a CPD-8505 fatty acid at the end of the tail .

name: N-pentylpantothenamide
annotation: N-pentylpantothenamide (N5-Pan) is an analogue of pantothenate in which the terminal carboxyl group has been condensed with a five-carbon alkyl amine to give an amide with a five-carbon alkyl chain. It is a potent inhibitor of E. coli growth. The compound and its derivatives are accepted with high efficiency (10-fold faster than pantothenate) by the last five enzymes of COA-PWY CoA biosynthesis pathway, forming CPD-26636, an analogue of CO-A . The main mechanism by which N5-Pan inhibits growth of E. coli is by binding of CPD-26636 to the EG12211 "PanZ protein", which results in loss of its ability to activate self-cleavage by the ASPDECARBOX-MONOMER "PanD protein", resulting in inhibition of B-ALANINE synthesis and as a result, a defficiency of CoA .

name: L-homocysteine thiolactone
annotation: Homocysteine editing by methionyl-tRNA synthetase results in the formation of homocysteine thiolactone. In addition to being cleared from the circulation by urinary excretion, homocysteine thiolactone is detoxified by the serum homocysteine thiolactonase/paraoxonase carried on high density lipoprotein, and inside cells by the enzyme known as EC-3.4.22.40 .

name: aztreonam
annotation: CPD-9235 Aztreonam is a U.S. Food and Drug Administration-approved monobactam in clinical use, and has proved efficacious in the clinical treatment of many multidrug-resistant infections, eradicating those caused by bacteria resistant to the most broadly active antibiotics, including meropenem, vancomycin, and imipenem . It is equipped with a third-generation cephalosporin side chain to improve activity against aerobic Gram-negative bacteria such as Pseudomonas species .

name: As3H3O6
annotation: This compound is a polymer of CPD-763 "arsenous acid", the latter being the predominant arsenic species at neutral pH . The polymer was proposed by to be a substrate of the arsenic:H+ antiporter in E.coli K-12.

name: 6-diazo-5-oxo-L-norleucine
annotation: CPD0-1627 "6-Diazo-5-oxo-L-norleucine" (DON) is the best-studied broadly active GLN antagonist, and is used as an inhibitor of enzymes that act on GLN, such as EC-3.5.1.2. The compound was originally described as a tumor-inhibitory antibiotic produced by an unidentified Streptomyces species isolated from Peruvian soil . In the same year it was also reported from TAX-66896 as a component of the tripeptide CPD-24748 . The compound was tested as a chemotherapeutic agent in several clinical studies ,but was never approved due to its toxicity . It is gaining renewed attention due to the recognition of the importance of glutaminolysis in cancer cells . The diazo group is a rare functional group that is present in some natural products, such as CPD-24753, CPD-24760, the Kinamycins kinamycins, and the Lomaiviticins lomaiviticins. The complete biosynthetic pathway of CPD-24748, including the production of CPD0-1627 from LYS, has been reported (see PWY-8293).

name: dicoumarol
annotation: Dicoumarol causes internal bleeding in livestock and is the forerunner of the warfarin group of medicinal anticoagulant .

name: tylosin
annotation: Tylosin is a macrolide type antibiotic produced by several species of bacteria. While the bacterium TAX-1906 is used for the commercial production of TYLOSIN , other species that produce it include strains of TAX-1927 and TAX-1912 . Tylosin has has a broad spectrum of activity against Gram-positive organisms and a limited range of Gram-negative organisms . It is extremely active against Mycoplasma species isolated from both mammalian and avian hosts . Like other macrolides, tylosin has a bacteriostatic effect caused by inhibition of protein synthesis through binding to the 50S subunit of the bacterial ribosome . Tylosin is used in veterinary medicine to treat bacterial infections in a wide range of species and is considered very safe . It has also been used as a growth promotant in some species , and as a treatment for colitis in companion animals . Tylosine is composed of of a 16-membered branched lactone ring (CPD-15957) and three sugars, CPD-13969, CPD-13844, and Mycinopyranose .

name: muscimol
annotation: CPD-22030 Muscimol is one of the principal psychoactive constituents of TAX-41956 and related species of mushrooms. It is a potent, selective agonist for the GABAA receptors with exceptionally high affinity to GABAA-delta receptors . GABAA receptors are widely distributed in the brain, and so when muscimol is administered, it alters neuronal activity in multiple regions including the cerebral cortex, hippocampus, and cerebellum, resulting in sedative-hypnotic, depressant and hallucinogenic psychoactivity. CPD-22030 Muscimol is produced by decarboxylation of CPD-23410.

name: peroxynitrite
annotation: Peroxynitrite is formed spontaneously by the reaction of NITRIC-OXIDE with SUPER-OXIDE. Peroxynitrite and DNIC "dinitrosyl iron complexes", also products of NITRIC-OXIDE, are known as reactive nitrogen species (RNS). The antimicrobial actions of NITRIC-OXIDE and reactive nitrogen species are considered as the most effective broad spectrum antimicrobial mechanism provided by the host resistance system operating in innate immunity .

name: albicidin
annotation: Albicidin is a hybrid polyketide-peptide antibiotic produced by the Gram-negative plant pathogen TAX-29447. It consists of six residues: methyl 4-coumarate, 4-aminobenzoate, β-cyano-L-alanine, a 2nd 4-aminobenzoate, 4-amino-2-hydroxy-3-methoxybenzoate, and a 2nd 4-amino-2-hydroxy-3-methoxybenzoate . Albicidin inhibits DNA gyrase at nanomolar concentration by stabilizing the cleavage complex. The native producer uses it to inhibit DNA gyrase in chloroplasts, resulting in inhibition of DNA replication leading to tissue necrosis and rapid plant death . However, albicidin has a general bactericidal activity towards fluoroquinolone-resistant Gram-negative pathogens. Albicidin employs a dual binding mechanism in which one end of the molecule obstructs the gyrase dimer interface while the other intercalates between the fragments of cleaved DNA substrate. As a result albicidin locks DNA gyrase, preventing it from religating DNA and completing its catalytic cycle .
